Proposed_Role: biological natural product expansion; high-complexity and medium-complexity bio structures
Key_Fields_To_Extract: name; SMILES; InChIKey; compound class; organism; literature reference; NPAtlas ID
Verification_Needs: check original reference for selected high-value records; nonbiotic search for simple metabolites
Risk_or_Caution: microbial natural products are biological occurrence evidence, not life-exclusive evidence
Source_Note: Use as first biological structure source because records are curated and organism-linked.

Source_Name: NPASS natural product activity and species source database
Proposed_Role: biological natural product expansion with species/source linkage
Key_Fields_To_Extract: name; structure; species source; reference; activity; NPASS ID
Verification_Needs: check record-level species source and reference; avoid activity-only imports
Risk_or_Caution: large database; not every natural product is unique or life-exclusive
Source_Note: Good complement to NPAtlas because it emphasizes species source and references.

Proposed_Role: broad natural product source-organism candidate pool
Key_Fields_To_Extract: name; structure; organism; reference; Wikidata/LOTUS IDs
Verification_Needs: confirm record-level references before promotion to A tier
Risk_or_Caution: aggregated records require careful provenance filtering
Source_Note: Useful for broad coverage, but should be sampled rather than imported wholesale.

Proposed_Role: large natural product chemical-space background and scaffold discovery
Key_Fields_To_Extract: name; SMILES; InChIKey; source database; organism/source metadata where present
Verification_Needs: filter for records with source evidence; avoid treating all records as equal evidence
Risk_or_Caution: very large and heterogeneous; may introduce source and scaffold leakage
Source_Note: Use for candidate discovery and scaffold diversity, not as a direct training set.

Proposed_Role: low-MA biological occurrence controls and endogenous metabolite structures
Key_Fields_To_Extract: name; SMILES/InChI; HMDB ID; biospecimen/location; references
Verification_Needs: targeted nonbiotic-origin search for every selected simple metabolite
Risk_or_Caution: many metabolites also occur abiotically or environmentally
Source_Note: Use conservatively as biological occurrence, not life-exclusive label.

Key_Fields_To_Extract: name; structure; ECMDB ID; pathway; references
Verification_Needs: nonbiotic-origin search for small metabolites; verify organism-specific occurrence
Source_Note: Good low-MA biological occurrence comparison set.

Proposed_Role: biological lipid structural diversity and high-MA biological candidates
Key_Fields_To_Extract: name; LM ID; class; SMILES; InChIKey; references
Verification_Needs: check source/reference and decide whether occurrence is organism-specific
Risk_or_Caution: fatty acids and simple lipids often have abiotic reports
Source_Note: Useful for high-complexity biological structures, but labels need care.

Proposed_Role: strong direct-returned-sample abiotic small molecule and biomolecule-related expansion
Key_Fields_To_Extract: name; formula; compound class; exact table; sample context; analytical method
Verification_Needs: map every imported molecule to exact supplementary table/row; avoid news-only sourcing
Risk_or_Caution: some structures may be tentative or formula-level rather than graph-level
Source_Note: High-priority because direct returned sample reduces terrestrial contamination ambiguity.

Proposed_Role: source discovery and plain-language context for Bennu 2025
Verification_Needs: use only to find primary papers; do not cite as molecule evidence
Source_Note: Useful as orientation, not database evidence.

Source_Name: Murchison/Murray/Tagish Lake nucleobases, sugars, and meteoritic soluble organics
Proposed_Role: meteorite abiotic expansion independent of Bennu/Ryugu
Key_Fields_To_Extract: name; formula; structure; meteorite; extraction method; exact table/page
Verification_Needs: confirm contamination assessment and exact analytical confidence across papers
Risk_or_Caution: meteorites have higher contamination risk than returned samples
Source_Note: Use as a source group; each paper should become a separate Source_ID when imported.

Proposed_Role: abiotic nucleobase candidates and biological-molecule false-positive controls
Key_Fields_To_Extract: name; structure; meteorite; isotopic/contamination evidence; exact location
Verification_Needs: prefer peer-reviewed primary records where available; check analytical certainty
Risk_or_Caution: older reports can have contamination debates
Source_Note: Use as a literature trail, then promote only primary verified records.

Source_Name: NASA Ames PAH IR Spectroscopic Database
Proposed_Role: high-complexity PAH and heteroatom-PAH structural pressure test
Key_Fields_To_Extract: name; formula; charge; structure; PAHdb ID; spectrum; computed/experimental flag
Verification_Needs: distinguish actual astrophysical detection, laboratory spectrum, and theoretical structure
Risk_or_Caution: not all PAHdb entries are observed abiotic molecules
Source_Note: Useful for high-MA nonbiological structural candidates.

Proposed_Role: structure and property validation for PAH/combustion/synthetic molecules
Key_Fields_To_Extract: name; formula; CAS; InChI; structure; spectra/useful identifiers
Verification_Needs: do not use NIST alone for origin labels; link to combustion/pyrolysis papers separately
Risk_or_Caution: chemical reference source, not origin evidence
Source_Note: Good for standardizing structures and identifiers.

Source_Name: CDMS Cologne Database for Molecular Spectroscopy
Proposed_Role: interstellar/circumstellar detected molecule expansion, mostly low-MA abiotic controls
Key_Fields_To_Extract: molecule; formula; isotopologue; spectroscopic tag; literature reference
Verification_Needs: confirm astronomical detection paper for selected molecules
Risk_or_Caution: structure may be trivial; origin label depends on detection context
Source_Note: Good independent abiotic source domain for small molecules.

Proposed_Role: astrochemical reaction species and formation-plausibility context
Key_Fields_To_Extract: species; formula; reactions; network source; references
Verification_Needs: use as formation context; pair with detection/source evidence when possible
Risk_or_Caution: reaction-network species are not necessarily observed molecules
Source_Note: Useful for nonbiotic formation mechanisms rather than final origin labels.

Proposed_Role: prebiotic experimental amino acids, amines, carboxylic acids, and small organics
Key_Fields_To_Extract: name; structure; experimental condition; exact table; analytical method
Verification_Needs: separate classic reports from modern reanalysis; exact experiment condition required
Risk_or_Caution: small molecules often overlap with biology and meteorites
Source_Note: Use for independent experimental abiotic evidence.

Key_Fields_To_Extract: name; structure; reaction pathway; condition; exact figure/table
Verification_Needs: record reaction context and whether product was isolated or proposed
Risk_or_Caution: pathway intermediates may be hypothetical or condition-dependent

Key_Fields_To_Extract: name; structure; catalyst/mineral; temperature; exact table/figure
Verification_Needs: confirm product identification level and avoid overgeneralizing complex mixtures
Risk_or_Caution: product lists may be group-level rather than exact structures
Source_Note: Use after returned-sample and natural-product pilot sources are stable.
